articles.title: Low intake of protein diet could help slow liver cancer, finds study
articles.summary: A new study shows that lowering protein intake could slow liver cancer in people with damaged livers. Researchers found that excess ammonia produced during protein metabolism can fuel tumour growth when the liver cannot properly remove it. In mice, a low-protein diet reduced ammonia levels, slowed tumour development and significantly improved survival.
articles.content: This article could not be fully loaded. Please visit the original source: https://www.moneycontrol.com/health-and-fitness/low-intake-of-protein-diet-could-help-slow-liver-cancer-finds-study-article-13856547.html

articles.title: Single receptor PAR1 controls opposing responses in blood vessels
articles.summary: Researchers at the University of California San Diego have uncovered how a single protein triggers two opposite responses in blood vessels - one inflammatory and one protective.

articles.title: Study links p-tau217 levels to long-term dementia risk in women
articles.summary: Researchers from the University of California San Diego have found that a novel blood-based biomarker can predict a woman's risk of developing dementia as many as 25 years before symptoms appear.

articles.title: Eating late at night? Study suggests stopping meals 3 hours before bed could benefit health
articles.summary: A new study suggests that when you eat may matter just as much as what you eat. Researchers say stopping meals three hours before bedtime could improve blood pressure, blood sugar, and overall metabolic health.

articles.title: Stroke survivors under 50 face greater cognitive and mental health challenges
articles.summary: Analysis of a large, nationally representative survey shows that stroke survivors under age 50 have more problems concentrating and running errands and experience more poor mental health days than older stroke survivors do.
articles.content: This article could not be fully loaded. Please visit the original source: https://www.news-medical.net/news/20260310/Stroke-survivors-under-50-face-greater-cognitive-and-mental-health-challenges.aspx

articles.title: Holding blood thinners for too long may jeopardize surgical recovery
articles.summary: Blood thinners are a common medication for much of the older adult population, prescribed to prevent blood clots that can cause adverse events like heart attack or stroke.

articles.title: Low-cost interventions reduce neonatal sepsis from Klebsiella pneumoniae
articles.summary: A new study found that a multifaceted infection prevention and control intervention could at least temporarily thwart outbreaks of infections from the Klebsiella pneumoniae bacterium, a leading cause of neonatal sepsis and mortality in Africa and South Asia.
